cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking about how the father reacted when he was laid off. The options are (A) was angry with his boss, (B) didn't care about it at all, (C) couldn't accept the fact easily, and (D) was as happy as usual. First, I need to refer back to the passage provided. Let me reread the relevant parts. The narrator comes home and finds his dad. The dad says he was laid off, and the narrator initially thinks he's joking. Then he notices the dad's expression and realizes it's true. The passage mentions that the dad was a hard worker and proud of his career, so being laid off affected his self-esteem. He was home all day, searching for jobs on the computer. The dad also asked his kids to spend less money. Later, the dad decides to start his own business. So, looking at the options: (A) angry with his boss – The passage doesn't mention the dad being angry. He just says he was laid off and starts looking for jobs. No mention of anger towards his boss. (B) didn't care – No, the opposite is true. The dad is clearly affected, as he's searching for jobs, the self-esteem issue, and the family had to make changes. (C) couldn't accept the fact easily – The narrator says that the dad tried to be optimistic but was down and his self-esteem was affected. The dad didn't just accept it easily. He went through a period of unemployment, searching for months, then decided to start a business. So it seems he had a hard time accepting it. (D) was as happy as usual – No, the dad's expression
